mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (currently 0.37%) reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a 20-trading-day horizon as long as the rate remains historically low.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.37 as of t-1mo, near historic lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX posts negative 20d returns in more than 55% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% within a 3-month window signals a deteriorating labor market that precedes falling consumer spending and corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as markets reprice recession probability.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% within a rolling 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.7 as of t-1mo, near cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of instances where the UNRATE threshold is triggered, or rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation) is deeply negative, the combined effect of near-zero nominal rates and elevated inflation creates strong liquidity-driven demand for equities as a real-asset hedge, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon. Neither series alone (rate level or inflation level) is sufficient; the joint negative real-rate condition is required.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.37 while CPIAUCSL=238.89 implying elevated inflation and deeply negative real rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between the real-rate spread and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 1-year window, or short-horizon returns are not significantly higher during negative real-rate periods versus positive real-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In an ultra-low interest rate environment, accelerating housing starts signal robust household formation and construction-sector demand that feeds through to broader consumer and equity market strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-trading-day horizon. This effect is specific to low-rate regimes and does not hold when policy rates are elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1172.0 thousand units as of t-2mo
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are not significantly higher (p>0.05) during HOUST acceleration episodes occurring within low-rate regimes compared to baseline, or the relationship reverses sign when tested in a subsequent rate-hiking regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in industrial production reflects expanding real output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue and earnings growth and translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day (quarterly) horizon on an always-on basis.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=103.5527 as of t-1mo, above pre-pandemic baseline
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trend growth and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns following INDPRO uptrends are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth accelerates simultaneously with a falling unemployment rate, the joint signal confirms genuine labor market tightening that boosts aggregate income and consumer spending power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-trading-day horizon. Payroll growth or unemployment decline alone, without the other confirming, does not produce a reliable signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=143894.0 thousand and UNRATE=4.7 as of t-1mo, both trending favorably
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE confirmation signal versus forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the signal underperforms either single-series baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month deceleration of retail sales below 0% growth signals an abrupt weakening in consumer demand that markets reprice quickly, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon following the release.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 55% of instances where RSAFS MoM growth is negative, or rank IC remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: At the onset of a monetary tightening cycle, when trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 6% while FEDFUNDS remains historically low, markets anticipate an aggressive rate-hike path that raises future discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL trailing 12-month inflation exceeds 6% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=238.89 indicating elevated inflation while FEDFUNDS=0.37 remains near zero
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of instances where the inflation-tightening trigger is active, or rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows spanning subsequent tightening episodes.
